majorArcana.name: The Fool
symbolism: Young man on cliff edge represents new beginning
White sun symbolizes consciousness and enlightenment
Mountains in background indicate future challenges
White rose in hand signifies purity and desire
Small dog as companion represents loyalty and instinct
Knapsack contains untapped potential
Colorful clothing shows optimism and naivety

divinatory.upright: Folly, mania, extravagance, intoxication, delirium, frenzy, bewrayment. New beginnings, having faith in the future, being inexperienced, not knowing what to expect, having beginner's luck, improvisation and believing in the universe.
divinatory.reversed: Negligence, absence, distribution, carelessness, apathy, nullity, vanity. Folly, recklessness, risk-taking, thoughtlessness.

majorArcana.name: The Magician
symbolism: Infinity symbol above head represents unlimited potential
Right hand pointing upward to heaven, left pointing to earth - as above, so below
Red robe symbolizes action and worldly experience
White undergarment represents spiritual purity
Four suit symbols on table - mastery of all elements (wands, cups, swords, pentacles)
Red roses and white lilies represent desire and pure thought
Yellow background symbolizes conscious mind and intellect

divinatory.upright: Skill, diplomacy, address, subtlety; sickness, pain, loss, disaster, snares of enemies; self-confidence, will; the Querent, if male. Power, concentration, action, consciousness.
divinatory.reversed: Physician, Magus, mental disease, disgrace, disquiet. Manipulation, poor planning, untapped talents.

majorArcana.name: The High Priestess
symbolism: Seated between black and white pillars (B and J) representing duality
Veil decorated with pomegranates behind her conceals mysteries
Crown with horns and globe represents three phases of moon
Blue robe flows like water symbolizing unconscious
Torah scroll labeled TORA represents divine law and hidden knowledge
Cross on breast shows balance of masculine/feminine
Crescent moon at feet connects to lunar mysteries

divinatory.upright: Secrets, mystery, the future as yet unrevealed; the woman who interests the Querent, if male; the Querent herself, if female; silence, tenacity; mystery, wisdom, science. Intuition, higher powers, the subconscious mind.
divinatory.reversed: Passion, moral or physical ardor, conceit, surface knowledge. Secrets, disconnected from intuition, withdrawal.

majorArcana.name: The Empress
symbolism: Seated on cushioned throne in abundant nature
Crown with twelve stars represents twelve months and zodiac
Venus symbol on heart-shaped shield shows love and beauty
Scepter represents dominion over life
Flowing robe suggests pregnancy and fertility
Wheat field symbolizes harvest and abundance
Waterfall and trees represent fertility of nature
Pomegranates on robe indicate fertility

divinatory.upright: Fruitfulness, action, initiative, length of days; the unknown, clandestine; also difficulty, doubt, ignorance. Abundance, nurturing, fertility, life creation.
divinatory.reversed: Light, truth, the unraveling of involved matters, public rejoicings; according to another reading, vacillation. Creative block, dependence on others.

majorArcana.name: The Emperor
symbolism: Stone throne decorated with ram heads (Aries symbol)
Red robe under armor shows passion controlled by structure
Ankh scepter represents life and masculine power
Golden orb in left hand symbolizes world domination
Barren mountain background shows solitary authority
Long white beard indicates wisdom and experience
Crown represents sovereignty and established power
Armor suggests protection and defensive strength

divinatory.upright: Stability, power, protection, realization; a great person; aid, reason, conviction; also authority and will. Leadership, father figure, structure, solid foundation.
divinatory.reversed: Benevolence, compassion, credit; also confusion to enemies, obstruction, immaturity. Domination, excessive control, rigidity, inflexibility.

majorArcana.name: The Hierophant
symbolism: Religious figure seated between two pillars like High Priestess
Triple crown represents three worlds - conscious, subconscious, superconscious
Raised right hand in blessing shows spiritual authority
Papal cross staff with three crossbars symbolizes trinity
Two acolytes kneeling represent exoteric and esoteric knowledge
Crossed keys at feet unlock spiritual mysteries
Red and white roses on acolytes' robes show passion and purity

divinatory.upright: Marriage, alliance, captivity, servitude; by another account, mercy and goodness; inspiration; the man to whom the Querent has recourse. Spiritual wisdom, religious beliefs, conformity, tradition.
divinatory.reversed: Society, good understanding, concord, overkindness, weakness. Personal beliefs, freedom, challenging the status quo.

majorArcana.name: The Lovers
symbolism: Naked man and woman represent Adam and Eve before the fall
Archangel Raphael above with arms outstretched in blessing
Sun shining behind angel symbolizes conscious partnership
Tree behind woman with serpent is Tree of Knowledge (temptation)
Tree behind man with flames is Tree of Life (passion)
Mountain represents challenges in relationship
Purple robe of angel shows spirituality
Clouds beneath angel suggest divine intervention

divinatory.upright: Attraction, love, beauty, trials overcome. Love, union, relationships, values alignment, choices.
divinatory.reversed: Failure, foolish designs. Another account speaks of marriage frustrated and contrarieties of all kinds. Disharmony, imbalance, misalignment of values.

majorArcana.name: The Chariot
symbolism: Armored warrior in chariot shows control and confidence
Starry crown and canopy represent celestial influences
Black and white sphinxes represent opposing forces controlled by will
Square on breastplate shows earth element and stability
Crescent moons on shoulders represent receptivity
Wand or scepter shows authority and command
City behind represents civilization and accomplishment
Blue background suggests emotional control

divinatory.upright: Succour, providence; also war, triumph, presumption, vengeance, trouble. Control, willpower, success, action, determination.
divinatory.reversed: Riot, quarrel, dispute, litigation, defeat. Lack of control, lack of direction, aggression.

majorArcana.name: Strength
symbolism: Woman gently closing lion's mouth shows inner strength over brute force
Infinity symbol above head represents unlimited spiritual power
White robe symbolizes purity and spiritual nature
Flowers in background and garland suggest natural harmony
Mountain in distance represents achievement through patience
Lion represents primal passions and instincts
Calm expression shows mastery through compassion
Gentle touch rather than force shows true power

divinatory.upright: Power, energy, action, courage, magnanimity; also complete success and honours. Strength, courage, persuasion, influence, compassion.
divinatory.reversed: Despotism, abuse of power, weakness, discord, disgrace. Self-doubt, weakness, insecurity, low energy.

majorArcana.name: The Hermit
symbolism: Old man with long beard stands alone on snowy mountain peak
Grey hooded robe represents invisibility and withdrawal
Lantern held high contains six-pointed star (Seal of Solomon)
Staff in left hand aids journey and provides support
Mountain top represents spiritual attainment through isolation
Snow suggests clarity and purity of thought
Looking down implies sharing wisdom with those below
Six-pointed star in lantern represents divine wisdom

divinatory.upright: Prudence, circumspection; also and especially treason, dissimulation, roguery, corruption. Soul searching, introspection, being alone, inner guidance.
divinatory.reversed: Concealment, disguise, policy, fear, unreasoned caution. Isolation, loneliness, withdrawal.

majorArcana.name: Wheel of Fortune
symbolism: Large wheel in center represents cycles and destiny
Hebrew letters YHVH on wheel spell name of God
Four creatures in corners - angel, eagle, lion, bull - represent fixed zodiac signs
Snake descending on left represents Set and material forces
Anubis ascending on right represents rising consciousness
Sphinx on top with sword represents riddles of existence
Alchemical symbols on wheel - mercury, sulfur, water, salt
Books held by figures suggest learning and wisdom

divinatory.upright: Destiny, fortune, success, elevation, luck, felicity. Good luck, karma, life cycles, destiny, a turning point.
divinatory.reversed: Increase, abundance, superfluity. Bad luck, negative external forces, out of control.

majorArcana.name: Justice
symbolism: Crowned figure seated between two pillars like High Priestess
Scales in left hand represent balanced judgment
Upright sword in right hand shows clarity and decisiveness
Purple veil behind suggests spiritual authority
Red robe indicates passion for truth
Green mantle represents growth through justice
Square on crown shows organized thought
Stern expression indicates impartiality

divinatory.upright: Equity, rightness, probity, executive; triumph of the deserving side in law. Justice, fairness, truth, cause and effect, law.
divinatory.reversed: Law in all its departments, legal complications, bigotry, bias, excessive severity. Unfairness, lack of accountability, dishonesty.

majorArcana.name: The Hanged Man
symbolism: Man suspended upside-down from T-shaped tree
Halo around head shows spiritual enlightenment through sacrifice
Peaceful expression indicates voluntary surrender
One leg crossed behind forms number 4 (stability through surrender)
Hands behind back suggest binding or acceptance
Blue tunic represents knowledge and calm
Red pants/tights show passion and life force
Yellow shoes and hair suggest mental and spiritual illumination

divinatory.upright: Wisdom, circumspection, discernment, trials, sacrifice, intuition, divination, prophecy. Suspension, restriction, letting go, sacrifice.
divinatory.reversed: Selfishness, the crowd, body politic. Delays, resistance, stalling, indecision.

majorArcana.name: Death
symbolism: Skeleton knight in black armor represents inevitable change
Black horse symbolizes death and unknown
White rose on black banner shows beauty and immortality
Fallen king shows death comes to all regardless of status
Pleading figures - king, child, maiden, bishop - show universal impact
Rising sun between towers indicates rebirth and new beginning
River in background represents flow between life and death
Five-petaled rose represents five senses transcended

divinatory.upright: End, mortality, destruction, corruption; also for a man, the loss of a benefactor; for a woman, many contrarieties; for a maid, failure of marriage projects. Endings, change, transformation, transition.
divinatory.reversed: Inertia, sleep, lethargy, petrifaction, somnambulism; hope destroyed. Resistance to change, stagnation, decay.

majorArcana.name: Temperance
symbolism: Angel with large wings represents higher self and protection
Pouring water between two cups shows alchemy and flow
One foot on land, one in water represents balance of conscious/subconscious
Triangle in square on chest shows fire within matter
Iris flowers represent hope and the rainbow bridge
Sun with crown rising between mountains shows enlightened consciousness
Path leading to mountains represents spiritual journey
Yellow background suggests conscious awareness

divinatory.upright: Economy, moderation, frugality, management, accommodation. Balance, moderation, patience, purpose.
divinatory.reversed: Things connected with churches, religions, sects, the priesthood, sometimes even the priest who will marry the Querent; also disunion, unfortunate combinations, competing interests. Imbalance, excess, self-healing, re-alignment.

majorArcana.name: The Devil
symbolism: Horned devil figure resembles Baphomet - symbol of material bondage
Inverted pentagram on forehead represents inverted spirituality
Bat wings indicate inability to fly toward light
Torch held downward brings fire and destruction
Man and woman chained loosely - bondage is voluntary
Horns and tails on humans show animal nature
Black background represents ignorance and unknowing
Chains are loose enough to remove - slavery is self-imposed

divinatory.upright: Ravage, violence, vehemence, extraordinary efforts, force, fatality; that which is predestined but is not for this reason evil. Shadow self, attachment, addiction, restriction, sexuality.
divinatory.reversed: Evil fatality, weakness, pettiness, blindness. Releasing limiting beliefs, exploring dark thoughts, detachment.

majorArcana.name: The Tower
symbolism: Lightning bolt striking tower represents sudden divine intervention
Crown blown off top shows ego and false constructs destroyed
Two figures falling headfirst show loss of control
Flames coming from windows indicate purification through destruction
Grey tower built on rocky mountain represents false structures on shaky foundation
22 flames represent 22 Hebrew letters and Major Arcana
Black background shows void and chaos
White lightning reveals truth through shock

divinatory.upright: Misery, distress, indigence, adversity, calamity, disgrace, deception, ruin. It is a card in particular of unforeseen catastrophe. Sudden change, upheaval, chaos, revelation, awakening.
divinatory.reversed: According to one account, the same in a lesser degree; also oppression, imprisonment, tyranny. Personal transformation, fear of change, averting disaster.

majorArcana.name: The Star
symbolism: Naked woman kneeling represents openness and vulnerability
Large eight-pointed star surrounded by seven smaller stars - hope and inspiration
Pouring water into pool and onto land nourishes conscious and unconscious
One foot on land, one in water shows balance of elements
Tree with bird represents thoughts taking flight
Pool represents subconscious mind
Mountains in background represent future achievements
Nudity shows nothing to hide, spiritual nakedness

divinatory.upright: Loss, theft, privation, abandonment; another reading says: hope and bright prospects. Hope, faith, purpose, renewal, spirituality.
divinatory.reversed: Arrogance, haughtiness, impotence. Lack of faith, despair, self-trust, disconnection.

majorArcana.name: The Moon
symbolism: Moon with face shows consciousness in the unconscious
Dog and wolf howling represent tamed and wild aspects of mind
Crayfish emerging from water symbolizes early stages of consciousness
Path between two towers leads to unknown
Towers represent known world and threshold to unconscious
Water represents subconscious and emotions
Mountains in distance show challenges ahead
Droplets falling represent manifestation from above

divinatory.upright: Hidden enemies, danger, calumny, darkness, terror, deception, occult forces, error. Illusion, fear, anxiety, subconscious, intuition.
divinatory.reversed: Instability, inconstancy, silence, lesser degrees of deception and error. Release of fear, repressed emotion, inner confusion.

majorArcana.name: The Sun
symbolism: Bright sun with face radiates warmth and consciousness
Naked child on white horse represents innocence and purity
Red banner shows energy and life force
Sunflowers facing sun represent natural growth toward light
Brick wall behind shows civilization and accomplishment
Child's nudity shows nothing to hide, authenticity
Straight sunbeams alternate with wavy ones - direct and reflected light
White horse symbolizes purity of thought

divinatory.upright: Material happiness, fortunate marriage, contentment. Success, positivity, fun, warmth, optimism, vitality.
divinatory.reversed: The same in a lesser sense. Temporary depression, lack of success, sadness.

majorArcana.name: Judgement
symbolism: Archangel Gabriel blowing trumpet announces resurrection
People rising from coffins represent rebirth and renewal
Man, woman, and child show family and universal experience
Cross on banner represents spiritual awakening
Mountain range in background represents challenges overcome
Grey skin of figures shows they come from death/shadow
Raised arms show receptivity to divine call
Coffins floating on water represent emotional depths transcended

divinatory.upright: Change of position, renewal, outcome. Another account specifies total loss though lawsuit. Judgement, rebirth, inner calling, absolution.
divinatory.reversed: Weakness, pusillanimity, simplicity; also deliberation, decision, sentence. Self-doubt, inner critic, ignoring the call.

majorArcana.name: The World
symbolism: Dancing figure in center represents cosmic consciousness and unity
Purple veil suggests wisdom and spiritual knowledge
Two wands in hands show balance of masculine/feminine
Laurel wreath symbolizes victory and achievement
Four figures in corners - angel, eagle, lion, bull - represent fixed signs and elements
Infinity symbol formed by purple scarves
Androgynous figure represents wholeness and integration
Green wreath indicates growth and natural cycles completed

divinatory.upright: Assured success, recompense, voyage, route, emigration, flight, change of place. Completion, integration, accomplishment, travel.
divinatory.reversed: Inertia, fixity, stagnation, permanence. Seeking personal closure, shortcuts, delays.
